description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running lengthwise across its entire width. The block is solid and robust, with sharply defined edges and a uniform, dark coloration that suggests it is made from a dense stone such as basalt or granite. The grooves are deep and regular, providing a textured, ribbed appearance that may serve both functional and aesthetic purposes. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the patterned top. The overall finish is matte with a slight natural stone texture, and there are no visible signs of significant weathering or surface damage. This type of block is commonly used as an urban obstacle barrier, such as in plazas, sidewalks, or as part of a modern landscape design.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and smooth, solid sides. The block has a matte, slightly textured finish and a robust, modern appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat face and the full width of the parallel grooves on the top surface.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the block's thickness visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooves from the opposite side with minimal visible difference.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the groove pattern and the block's solid, rectangular form.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a modern obstacle barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as part of a barrier line
